title: Agreement to extend Hokuriku Shinkansen through the Obama-Kyoto route with new Katsuragawa station in Kyoto
summary: The government and political parties agree on the final route for the bullet train extension, which will bring service to Japan's west coast and improve regional connectivity.

title: Trump withdraws 20% tariff on transit through the Strait of Hormuz as oil prices surge
summary: The United States revokes the controversial measure to tax ships passing through the strait, while Middle East tensions drive oil volatility and Japan seeks to diversify its imports.

title: Extreme heat wave in Japan leaves dozens hospitalized and record temperatures in over 140 locations
summary: Authorities issue heatstroke warnings across much of the country, with thermometers exceeding 38°C in several regions and Kyoto city recording its hottest day of the year.

title: Japanese government debates reform of Imperial House law allowing abdication and adoption to ensure succession
summary: The special Senate committee postpones the vote on the bill that would allow emperors to abdicate and women to retain their royal status after marriage, amid criticism over the male succession tradition.

title: Japanese influencer Miyazaki Rika sentenced for tax evasion of 150 million yen
summary: The Tokyo court hands down a suspended sentence for the well-known internet figure, who had hidden income from advertising and events.

title: The 175th Akutagawa and Naoki literary awards announce their winners among works depicting social transformation
summary: The jury selects the award-winning works in the prestigious Japanese literature contests, with special attention to narratives about the working world and identity.

title: Spain defeats France 2-0 and advances to World Cup final for the first time in 12 years
summary: The Spanish team, led by an inspired Lamine Yamal and a solid defense, secures its place in the tournament's decisive match.

title: Argentina and England face each other in the World Cup semifinals 24 years after their last match
summary: The match revives the historic rivalry between both teams, with Lionel Messi and Harry Kane as main figures.

title: Nichirei computer glitch causes product shortages in supermarkets and temporary closure of KFC restaurants
summary: The disruption of the company's logistics system affects numerous retailers and fast-food chains across the country.

title: Event production company ordered to pay 250 million yen for involvement in Tokyo 2020 bid rigging
summary: The court issues a ruling in the corruption scandal of the Olympic Games, which also involves other companies and executives.

title: Murderer of woman live-streaming on internet in Takadanobaba sentenced to 16 years in prison
summary: The defendant fatally stabbed the victim while she was live-streaming on the street, in a case that shocked Japan.

title: 6-year-old girl dies hit by car while heading to school in Komaki, Aichi
summary: The driver, a 40-year-old man, is arrested on the spot while the school community mourns the loss of the child.

title: Tokyo Disney Resort to increase maximum ticket price to 12,400 yen starting October
summary: The theme park implements its largest fare increase to cope with rising operating costs and growing demand.

title: Japanese player Munetaka Murakami debuts in MLB All-Star Game as Hideki Matsui praises his talent
summary: Murakami becomes the first Japanese player to participate in the All-Star Game since Ichiro Suzuki, and receives praise from legend Matsui.

title: Nikkei index rises more than 1,000 points driven by ASML results and optimism in artificial intelligence
summary: The Tokyo stock market records its biggest gain in months, with investors reacting positively to the Dutch chipmaker's earnings and the prospects of the technology sector.

title: Yen weakness reduces foreign exchange trading volume and changes the outlook for BOJ rate hike
summary: FX traders stay on the sidelines amid central bank intervention, while the fall of the Japanese currency alters monetary policy expectations.

title: Japan records record increase in karoshi cases and 15% of doctors exceed 960 overtime hours per year
summary: Authorities report a continuous increase in compensation for work-related accidents due to stress and overwork, while the health sector denounces unsustainable conditions.

title: More than half of Japanese households report financial difficulties and single-child households exceed 50% for the first time
summary: A survey by the Ministry of Health reveals that 53% of families are going through financial problems, while demographic change accelerates the trend toward smaller families.

title: World Cup could extend half-time break to 30 minutes and implement mandatory hydration breaks
summary: FIFA studies modifying the regulations for the next edition of the tournament, while clubs and sponsors assess the commercial impact of the new rules.

title: U.S. pharmaceutical company reports promising results in mid-stage clinical trial of new Alzheimer's drug
summary: The company announces that its experimental drug succeeds in slowing cognitive decline in patients with the disease, opening the door to possible accelerated approval.
